---
title: "Overview"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Settings
data_dir = Path("../data/processed")

# Try to load data
try:
    forecasts = pd.read_parquet(data_dir / "forecasts.parquet")
    features = pd.read_parquet(data_dir / "features.parquet")
except:
    forecasts = pd.DataFrame()
    features = pd.DataFrame()

```

## Key Metrics

```{python}
#| echo: false
if len(forecasts) > 0:
    num_fixtures = len(forecasts)
    avg_attendance = forecasts["predicted_attendance"].mean()
    max_attendance = forecasts["predicted_attendance"].max()
    last_updated = forecasts["forecast_timestamp"].iloc[0] if len(forecasts) > 0 else "N/A"

    col1, col2, col3, col4 = [None] * 4

    print(f"""
    - **Next {num_fixtures} Fixtures Forecasted** • Average: {avg_attendance:,.0f} • Peak: {max_attendance:,.0f}
    - **Model:** LightGBM • **Last Updated:** {last_updated[:10]}
    - **Features:** 14 engineered • **Seasons:** 2020-2024
    """)
else:
    print("⏳ No forecasts yet. Run the pipeline to generate predictions.")

```

## Top 5 Highest-Attendance Fixtures

```{python}
#| echo: false
if len(forecasts) > 0:
    top_5 = forecasts.nlargest(5, "predicted_attendance")[
        ["date", "home_team", "away_team", "predicted_attendance", "upper_bound"]
    ].copy()
    top_5["date"] = pd.to_datetime(top_5["date"]).dt.strftime("%b %d, %Y")
    top_5["predicted_attendance"] = top_5["predicted_attendance"].round(0).astype(int)
    top_5["upper_bound"] = top_5["upper_bound"].round(0).astype(int)
    top_5.columns = ["Date", "Home", "Away", "Predicted", "Upper Bound"]

    fig = go.Figure(data=[go.Table(
        header=dict(values=list(top_5.columns), fill_color='#457B9D', font=dict(color='white')),
        cells=dict(values=[top_5[col] for col in top_5.columns], fill_color='lavender')
    )])
    fig.update_layout(height=300, margin=dict(l=0, r=0, t=0, b=0))
    fig.show()
else:
    print("No forecast data available")

```

## Recent Match Performance

```{python}
#| echo: false
if len(features) > 10:
    # Last 10 finished matches
    recent = features.tail(10).copy()
    recent["predicted_attendance"] = recent["attendance_pct"] * recent["stadium_capacity"]

    fig = px.bar(recent, x="date", y="predicted_attendance",
                 color="home_team", title="Attendance: Last 10 Matches",
                 labels={"predicted_attendance": "Attendance", "date": "Date"},
                 hover_data={"home_team": True, "away_team": True})
    fig.update_layout(height=400, showlegend=False)
    fig.show()
else:
    print("Not enough historical data yet")

```

## Team Average Forecasted Attendance

```{python}
#| echo: false
if len(forecasts) > 0:
    team_stats = []
    for team in set(list(forecasts["home_team"]) + list(forecasts["away_team"])):
        home_matches = forecasts[forecasts["home_team"] == team]
        away_matches = forecasts[forecasts["away_team"] == team]
        avg = (home_matches["predicted_attendance"].mean() if len(home_matches) > 0 else 0 +
               away_matches["predicted_attendance"].mean() if len(away_matches) > 0 else 0) / 2
        team_stats.append({"team": team, "avg_attendance": avg})

    team_df = pd.DataFrame(team_stats).sort_values("avg_attendance", ascending=True).tail(10)

    fig = px.barh(team_df, x="avg_attendance", y="team",
                  title="Top 10 Teams by Avg. Forecasted Attendance",
                  labels={"avg_attendance": "Average Attendance"})
    fig.update_layout(height=400, showlegend=False)
    fig.show()
else:
    print("No data available")

```
